objects.name: Analytic 1837
objects.description: Correlates (1) application registration or activation of broadcast receivers tied to system or app-generated intents, (2) event-triggered execution while the application is not in the foreground, and (3) immediate follow-on actions such as network communication or data access. The defender observes a causal chain where an external event (e.g., BOOT_COMPLETED, SMS_RECEIVED, USER_PRESENT, CONNECTIVITY_CHANGE) triggers application execution that bypasses normal user-driven lifecycle expectations, followed by background processing or outbound activity.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application registers or invokes broadcast receiver via registerReceiver() or manifest-declared receiver + intent filter tied to system or app events

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Time correlation window between broadcast event and subsequent execution or network activity
List of broadcast intents considered high-risk (e.g., BOOT_COMPLETED, SMS_RECEIVED)
Baseline of legitimate applications expected to use broadcast receivers for these intents
Determines whether execution without foreground presence increases detection confidence
